page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration provides 372.0 GB of RAM, equating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highmem-48 demonstrates strong performance across multiple domains. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2208.0, alongside high marks in image and text processing. Multi-threaded performance is also robust, with a Passmark CPU score of 57593.71 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17983.0. For memory-intensive tasks, the instance delivers a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 3216.81 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read bandwidth drops to 144.15 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform well, highlighted by a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 34.04 GB/sec and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 284.57 GB/sec.

The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and software compilation, as evidenced by its strong Passmark database score of 22234.19 and Geekbench clang compilation score of 41626.0. It also supports light machine learning tasks, achieving strong prompt processing speeds across small and medium LLM models, though text generation on large 70B models is weak at 2.74 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The absence of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, representing a potential tradeoff for I/O-heavy database applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor, it delivers 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, supported by 372.0 GB of RAM and a 260 MB L3 cache. The architecture includes advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench tests, text processing, and image processing, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and database operations. While memory bandwidth is high at 3216.81 GB/sec for cached small blocks, uncached large-block writes are limited to 61.96 GB/sec. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is optimized for memory-heavy applications, enterprise databases, and CPU-based inference workloads that benefit from high memory density and dedicated compute resources without requiring local NVMe speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs, 372 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs and 372 GB RAM, built for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise databases and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM providing 7.75 GB per core
Intel Xeon 8581C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core database capabilities
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Optimized for memory-heavy applications, software compilation, and database operations
